77de43cee219.title: Publish opening hours — you're invisible to 'open now' searches
77de43cee219.description: No opening hours listed. Every customer filtering by 'open now' on Google Maps will never see you. This takes 2 minutes to fix.
77de43cee219.management_implication: This is a zero-effort, high-impact fix.

4b1e8699e047.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
4b1e8699e047.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
4b1e8699e047.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

a8568d39c47e.title: Build review volume — you lack statistical credibility
a8568d39c47e.description: Only 13 Google reviews. A single bad review moves your average visibly. Encourage satisfied customers to leave reviews.

5d7063711294.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
5d7063711294.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

0fd384622123.title: Strong rating masks weak competitive position
0fd384622123.description: Your 4.8/5 Google rating feels dominant but you sit below the local median (P25.0). Peer venues achieve similar or better ratings — in this market, 4.5 is table stakes, not a differentiator. Don't mistake the number for an advantage.
0fd384622123.management_implication: The rating looks good in isolation but not in context.

f033b8cea397.title: Ranked #4 of 5 locally — peers are ahead
f033b8cea397.description: At percentile 25.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.
